content.query:
select * from inceptez_salesdb.dbo.orders_delta;

select * from inceptezdb.dbo.ext_bankdata;

--https
select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'https://batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/Unemployment.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1;

--abfss
select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/Unemployment.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1;


--header row as column names(HEADER_ROW)

select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/Unemployment.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1;

--specific columns
select [line number], state,gender,employed,unemployed from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/Unemployment.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1;


--using alias
select d1.[line number], d1.state,d1.gender,d1.employed,unemployed from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/Unemployment.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as d1;

--filter data
select d1.[line number], d1.state,d1.gender,d1.employed,unemployed from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/Unemployment.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as d1 where state in ('CA','TX','NY');

--group by
select d1.state,count(*) as cnt from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/Unemployment.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as d1 where state in ('CA','TX','NY') group by state

--with clause

select  * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/Unemployment.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,    
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
)
WITH(
    [Line Number] INT,
    [Month] varchar(20)
) as d1;



select  * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/Unemployment.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    FIRSTROW = 2,    
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
)
WITH(
    [Line Number] INT,
    [Month] varchar(20)
) as d1;

--without header, setting the column names using WITH clause
select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/txns',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) 
WITH
(
    txnid int,
    txndate varchar(20),
    custid varchar(10),
    amount float,
    category varchar(100),
    product varchar(100),
    city varchar(100),
    state varchar(100),
    ptype varchar(10)
)
as txns;


-- define cardinal or column position 
select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/txns',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) 
WITH
(
    txnid int 1,
    state varchar(100) 8
)
as txns;


-- field terminator 
select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/txns',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = ',',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) 
WITH
(
    txnid int 1,
    state varchar(100) 8
)
as txns;

use inceptezdb;

SELECT * FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/txns',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = ',',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) 
WITH
(
    -- Format: [Column Name] [Data Type] [Column Index/Ordinal] [Collation]
    txnid int 1,
    state varchar(100) COLLATE Latin1_General_100_CI_AS_KS_SC_UTF8 8
)
as txns;

--set collate at database level
alter database inceptezdb collate Latin1_General_100_CI_AS_KS_SC_UTF8


-- select data without collate
use inceptezdb;
SELECT * FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/txns',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = ',',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) 
WITH
(
    -- Format: [Column Name] [Data Type] [Column Index/Ordinal] [Collation]
    txnid int 1,
    state varchar(100) 8
)
as txns;

--JSON data
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'https://batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/orders.json',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',  -- trick to read raw JSON
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'
) 
WITH (
    jsondata NVARCHAR(MAX)
)
AS rows;




--parse json data

SELECT JSON_VALUE(jsondata,'$.order_id') as order_id
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'https://batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/orders.json',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',  -- trick to read raw JSON
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'
) 
WITH (
    jsondata NVARCHAR(MAX)
)
AS rows;



---

SELECT order_id,customer, amount,city
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'https://batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/orders.json',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',  
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'  -- This is the missing piece
) 
WITH (
    jsondata NVARCHAR(MAX) 
) as src
CROSS APPLY OPENJSON(jsondata)
WITH 
(
    order_id INT '$.order_id',
    customer VARCHAR(100) '$.customer',
    amount FLOAT '$.amount',
    city VARCHAR(100) '$.city'
)
AS rows;

---create data source
use inceptezdb;

DROP  EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE raw_container;

CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE raw_container
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'https://batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/'
);


select * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'Unemployment.csv',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'raw_container',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as data1;


--create external file format

CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT synapse_csv_format
WITH
(
    FORMAT_TYPE = DELIMITEDTEXT
    FORMAT_OPTIONS (
        FIELD_TERMINATOR = ',',
        FIRST_ROW = 2
    )
);

--create external table

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ext_unemployement
(
    line_number int,
    month varchar(10)
)
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'Unemployment.csv',
    DATA_SOURCE = raw_container,
    FILE_FORMAT = synapse_csv_format
);

select * from ext_unemployement

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ext_txns
(
    txnid int,
    txndate varchar(20),
    custid varchar(10),
    amount float,
    category varchar(100),
    product varchar(100),
    city varchar(100),
    state varchar(100),
    ptype varchar(10)
)
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'txns',
    DATA_SOURCE = raw_container,
    FILE_FORMAT = synapse_csv_format
);

select * from ext_txns;

    BULK 'https://batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/custdata.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,    
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as d1;

select  * from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'https://inceptezadlsbankdata.dfs.core.windows.net/bankdata/bank_txns.csv',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,    
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as d1;

    BULK 'bank_txns.csv',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'bankdata_source',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,    
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) as d1;

CREATE MASTER KEY ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'AzureInceptez#1234'

DROP  DATABASE SCOPED CREDENTIAL creds;

CREATE DATABASE SCOPED CREDENTIAL creds
WITH IDENTITY = 'SHARED ACCESS SIGNATURE',
SECRET = 'sv=2025-11-05&ss=bfqt&srt=sco&sp=rwdlacupyx&se=2026-04-30T09:52:56Z&st=2026-04-08T01:37:56Z&spr=https&sig=6obrqPIL87OdCP5Q3RQtWiB4qzWJ4gkurmUuaCVQQgs%3D'

-- 1. Drop the existing data source so you can recreate it
DROP EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE bankdata_source;
GO

-- 2. Recreate with the 'dfs' endpoint AND the container name included
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE bankdata_source
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'https://inceptezadlsbankdata.dfs.core.windows.net/bankdata/', 
    CREDENTIAL = creds
);

GO

-- 3. Query just the file name (or folder/file if it's inside a subfolder)
SELECT * FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'bank_txns.csv', -- Removed 'bankdata/' since it's now in the data source
    DATA_SOURCE = 'bankdata_source',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,    
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) 
WITH
(
    txn_id VARCHAR(100)
) as d1;


---- DB Scoped Credentials using Managed Identity


--Step1: Create credentials

CREATE DATABASE SCOPED CREDENTIAL creds_mi
WITH IDENTITY = 'Managed Identity';

--step2: Goto storage account, provide "Storage Blob data contributor" role for the synapse ws managed identity

--Step3: Create external data source

CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE bankdata_source_mi
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'https://inceptezadlsbankdata.dfs.core.windows.net/bankdata/', 
    CREDENTIAL = creds_mi
);

--Step4: Query the file
SELECT * FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'bank_txns.csv', 
    DATA_SOURCE = 'bankdata_source_mi',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = TRUE,    
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) 
WITH
(
    txn_id VARCHAR(100)
) as d1;

---------------Create External Table----------------------------

--Step1: Create external data source = bankdata_source_mi
--Step2: Create external file format = synapse_csv_format
--Step3: Create external table

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ext_bankdata
(
    txn_id VARCHAR(10),
	txn_date VARCHAR(10),
	cust_id VARCHAR(10),
	amount FLOAT,
	branch VARCHAR(50),
	region VARCHAR(100),
	payment_type VARCHAR(10)
)
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'bank_txns.csv',
    DATA_SOURCE = bankdata_source_mi,
    FILE_FORMAT = synapse_csv_format
);

select * from ext_bankdata;


-- To get the list of external file format
SELECT * FROM sys.external_file_formats;

-- To get the list of external data source
SELECT * FROM sys.external_data_sources;

--To get the list of external tables
SELECT * FROM sys.external_tables;

--To get the list of columns in external table
SELECT * FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS where TABLE_NAME='ext_txns';

--To get the size of data processed
SELECT * FROM sys.dm_external_data_processed;

--CTAS
Create Table extdata AS
select * from ext_bankdata

--Create Dedicated SQL Pool

CREATE DATABASE inceptez_retaildb
(
    EDITION = 'DataWarehouse',
    SERVICE_OBJECTIVE = 'DW100c'
);

CREATE SCHEMA bank_schema;

Create table bank_schema.bank_txns
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN,  -- distributes data evenly across all nodes
    CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX  --large amount of data, compress and best of performance
);

--supports all CRUD(create,read,update,delete) operations

select * from bank_schema.bank_txns;


INSERT INTO bank_schema.bank_txns (txnid, txndate, custid, amt, branch, region)
VALUES 
(1001, '2026-04-01', 'CUST001', 1500.50, 'T. Nagar', 'Chennai');

VALUES 
(1002, '2026-04-02', 'CUST002', 2750.00, 'Adyar', 'Chennai');

VALUES 
(1003, '2026-04-02', 'CUST003', 500.25, 'Jayanagar', 'Bangalore');
INSERT INTO bank_schema.bank_txns (txnid, txndate, custid, amt, branch, region)
VALUES 
(1004, '2026-04-03', 'CUST001', 3200.00, 'T. Nagar', 'Chennai');

VALUES 
(1005, '2026-04-05', 'CUST004', 120.00, 'Hitech City', 'Hyderabad');


select * from bank_schema.bank_txns;

--update
UPDATE bank_schema.bank_txns set amt = 2000 where branch = 'Jayanagar'

select * from bank_schema.bank_txns;


--delete
DELETE FROM bank_schema.bank_txns where txnid=1005;

select * from bank_schema.bank_txns;


--HASH

Create table bank_schema.bank_txns_hash
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX,  --large amount of data, compress and best of performance
    DISTRIBUTION = HASH(region)  -- distributes data evenly across all nodes
);

--replicate

Create table bank_schema.bank_txns_replicate
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX,  --large amount of data, compress and best of performance
    DISTRIBUTION = REPLICATE  
);


--heap index
Create table bank_schema.bank_txns_heap_indx
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    HEAP, 
    DISTRIBUTION = REPLICATE  
);

--clustered index

Create table bank_schema.bank_txns_cluster_indx
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    CLUSTERED INDEX(region), 
    DISTRIBUTION = REPLICATE  
);

select * from INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES


/*

Row oriented data storage 
txnid,txndate
101,2026-01-01
102,2026-01-01
103,2026-01-01

Column oriented data storage
101,102,103
2026-01-01,2026-01-01,2026-01-01


*/

CREATE TABLE bank_schema.customerdata(
    custid INT,
    fname varchar(100),
    lname varchar(100),
    age int,
    prof varchar(100)
)
WITH
(
     CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX,
     DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN
);


COPY INTO bank_schema.customerdata
FROM 'https://batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/custdata.csv'
WITH(
    FILE_TYPE = 'CSV',
    FIRSTROW = 2
);

select * from bank_schema.customerdata;


Create table bank_schema.bank_txns_adls
(
    txnid varchar(20),
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX,  --large amount of data, compress and best of performance
    DISTRIBUTION = HASH(region)  -- distributes data evenly across all nodes
);

--reading data from external storage account

COPY INTO bank_schema.bank_txns_adls
FROM 'https://inceptezadlsbankdata.dfs.core.windows.net/bankdata/bank_txns.csv'
WITH(
    FILE_TYPE = 'CSV',
    FIRSTROW = 2,
    CREDENTIAL = (IDENTITY = 'Managed Identity')

);

select * from bank_schema.bank_txns_adls;

--Normal View

CREATE VIEW bank_schema.vw_banktxndata
AS
select * from bank_schema.bank_txns where region = 'Chennai';

select * from bank_schema.vw_banktxndata;

--Materialized View
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW bank_schema.vw_banktxndata_m
WITH (DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN)
AS
SELECT 
    region, 
    SUM(amt) AS total_amt,
    COUNT_BIG(*) AS txncount,      -- Counts all rows in the group
    COUNT_BIG(amt) AS amt_count    -- Counts only non-null values of 'amt'
FROM bank_schema.bank_txns 
GROUP BY region;

select * from  bank_schema.vw_banktxndata_m;

select  region, SUM(amt) AS total_amt from bank_schema.bank_txns GROUP BY region;

source: spark.sql("DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS hranalytics_db CASCADE")

#Step 1: Create Database

spark.sql("CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS hranalytics_db")

spark.sql("""CREATE TABLE hranalytics_db.dim_employee (
   emp_id STRING,
   name STRING,
   dept STRING,
   role STRING,
   location STRING,
   hire_date DATE,
   salary DOUBLE,
   status STRING,
   start_date DATE,
   end_date DATE,
   is_current BOOLEAN
)""")

#Step 2: Define Path
base_path = "abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/hr-rawsource/"

#Step 3: Read CSV Files
employee_df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(base_path + "employee.csv")

performance_df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(base_path + "performance.csv")

survey_df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(base_path + "survey.csv")

exit_df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(base_path + "exit.csv")

#Step 4: Add Bronze Metadata Columns
from pyspark.sql.functions import current_timestamp, lit

def add_bronze_columns(df, source_name):
    return df.withColumn("ingestion_time", current_timestamp()) \
             .withColumn("source_file", lit(source_name))

employee_df = add_bronze_columns(employee_df, "employee.csv")
performance_df = add_bronze_columns(performance_df, "performance.csv")
survey_df = add_bronze_columns(survey_df, "survey.csv")
exit_df = add_bronze_columns(exit_df, "exit.csv")

#Step 5: Write to Bronze Tables (Delta)
employee_df.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.bronze_employee")

performance_df.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.bronze_performance")

survey_df.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.bronze_survey")

exit_df.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.bronze_exit")

#Step 6: Validate
spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hranalytics_db.bronze_employee").show()
spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hranalytics_db.bronze_performance").show()
spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hranalytics_db.bronze_survey").show()
spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hranalytics_db.bronze_exit").show()

# Sales Data Analysis using Spark

## Objective
Analyze sales trends

### Steps
- Load data from ADLS
- Transform using Spark
- Generate KPIs

### Code

```python
df = spark.read.csv("abfss://container/file.csv")
print("Hello") 
print("World")
```

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession

# Create a SparkSession
spark1 = SparkSession.builder.appName("synapseworkouts").getOrCreate()
data = [
    (1, "Rajesh", 25),
    (2, "Vimal", 30),
    (3, "Charlie", 35),
    (4, "David", 40)
]

df = spark.createDataFrame(data, ["id", "name", "age"])
df.show()

#Read from Data Lake
df = spark.read.format("csv").option("inferschema",True).load("abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/txns") \
.toDF("txnid","txndate","custid","amount","category","product","city","state","paytype")
df.printSchema()
df.show()
df1 = df.filter("state = 'Texas' and paytype = 'credit'")
df1.show()

from pyspark.sql.functions import count,sum,round
df1 = df.groupBy("state").agg(count("txnid").alias("TotalTrans"),round(sum("amount"),2).alias("TotalSales"))
df1.show()

#Write into ADLS
df1.write.format("parquet").save("abfss://raw@batch25synapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/salesreport")
print("Data Written into Data Lake")

### Objective of Silver Layer
- Clean nulls & invalid data
- Standardize column names & formats
- Deduplicate records
- Join datasets for analytics-ready structure

#Step 1: Read Bronze Tables
employee_df = spark.read.table("hranalytics_db.bronze_employee")
performance_df = spark.read.table("hranalytics_db.bronze_performance")
survey_df = spark.read.table("hranalytics_db.bronze_survey")
exit_df = spark.read.table("hranalytics_db.bronze_exit")

#Step 2: Standardize Column Names
from pyspark.sql.functions import col

def standardize_columns(df):
    for c in df.columns:
        df = df.withColumnRenamed(c, c.strip().lower().replace(" ", "_"))        
    
    #drop columns
    df = df.drop("ingestion_time", "source_file")
    return df

employee_df = standardize_columns(employee_df)
performance_df = standardize_columns(performance_df)
survey_df = standardize_columns(survey_df)
exit_df = standardize_columns(exit_df)

#Step 3: Data Cleaning
# Employee Cleaning
from pyspark.sql.functions import when

employee_clean = employee_df.dropDuplicates(["emp_id"]) \
    .withColumn("salary", col("salary").cast("double")) \
    .fillna({
        "dept": "Unknown",
        "role": "Unknown"
    }) \
    .filter(col("emp_id").isNotNull())

#Performance Cleaning
performance_clean = performance_df.dropDuplicates() \
    .withColumn("rating", col("rating").cast("int")) \
    .filter(col("emp_id").isNotNull())

#Survey Cleaning (eNPS prep)
survey_clean = survey_df.dropDuplicates() \
    .withColumn("survey_score", col("survey_score").cast("int")) \
    .withColumn("enps_category",
        when(col("survey_score") >= 9, "Promoter")
        .when(col("survey_score") >= 5, "Passive")
        .otherwise("Detractor")
    )

#Exit Data Cleaning
exit_clean = exit_df.dropDuplicates() \
    .filter(col("emp_id").isNotNull()) \
    .withColumnRenamed("reason", "exit_reason")

employee_clean.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("overwriteSchema", "true") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.silver_employee")

performance_clean.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("overwriteSchema", "true") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.silver_performance")

survey_clean.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("overwriteSchema", "true") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.silver_survey")

exit_clean.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("overwriteSchema", "true") \
    .saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.silver_exit")
### Read data from external storage account using Managed Identity

#Read from data lake(External Storage) using Managed Identity

#Provide Storage blob data contributor access to the synapse workspace idenitity

# Ensure the path matches the account name above
path = "abfss://bankdata@inceptezadlsbankdata.dfs.core.windows.net/bank_txns.csv"

df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(path)

#Step 1: Read Tables
from pyspark.sql.functions import current_date, lit, col

df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_employee").alias("src")
dim_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.dim_employee").alias("tgt")

#Step 2: Detect Changed Records
changed_df = df.join(dim_df, col("src.emp_id") == col("tgt.emp_id")) \
   .filter(
       (col("tgt.is_current") == True) & (
           (col("src.salary") != col("tgt.salary")) |
           (col("src.role") != col("tgt.role")) |
           (col("src.dept") != col("tgt.dept")) |
           (col("src.location") != col("tgt.location"))
       )
   ).select("src.*")

#Step 3: Detect New Employees (Insert)
new_df = df.join(dim_df, "emp_id", "left_anti")

#Step 4: Expire Old Records
expired_df = dim_df.alias("tgt") \
   .join(changed_df.alias("src"), "emp_id") \
   .filter(col("tgt.is_current") == True) \
   .select("tgt.*") \
   .withColumn("end_date", current_date()) \
   .withColumn("is_current", lit(False))
expired_df.printSchema()

#Step 5: Prepare New Records (for both changed + new)
insert_df = changed_df.union(new_df) \
   .withColumn("start_date", current_date()) \
   .withColumn("end_date", lit(None)) \
   .withColumn("is_current", lit(True))
insert_df.printSchema()

#Final Merge Logic
# Keep unchanged records
unchanged_df = dim_df.alias("tgt") \
   .join(changed_df.alias("src"), "emp_id", "left_anti")
unchanged_df.printSchema()

# Final dataset
final_df = unchanged_df.union(expired_df).union(insert_df)

# Write to temp
final_df.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.employee_tmp")

#Write back
df_tmp = spark.table("hranalytics_db.employee_tmp")
df_tmp.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.dim_employee")

#Step 1: Read Silver Tables

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, when, lit

# Read Silver tables
emp_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_employee")
perf_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_performance")
survey_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_survey")
exit_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.silver_exit")

#Step 2: Read Current Dimension (SCD2)
#Always use only current records
dim_df = spark.table("hranalytics_db.dim_employee") \
              .filter(col("is_current") == True)

#Step 3: Join All Sources
fact_df = dim_df.alias("d") \
    .join(perf_df.alias("p"), col("d.emp_id") == col("p.emp_id"), "left") \
    .join(survey_df.alias("s"), col("d.emp_id") == col("s.emp_id"), "left") \
    .join(exit_df.alias("e"), col("d.emp_id") == col("e.emp_id"), "left")


#Step 4: Derive Metrics Columns

fact_df = fact_df.select(
    col("d.emp_id").alias("emp_sk"),
    col("p.rating"),
    col("s.survey_score"),
    col("p.review_date"),
    col("s.survey_date"),
    col("e.exit_date"),
    
    # Attrition Flag
    when(col("e.emp_id").isNotNull(), lit(1)).otherwise(lit(0)).alias("attrition_flag"),
    
    # Engagement Bucket
    when(col("s.survey_score") >= 8, "High")
    .when(col("s.survey_score") >= 5, "Medium")
    .otherwise("Low").alias("engagement_level"),
    
    # Risk Flag (Early Churn Detection)
    when((col("p.rating") <= 2) & (col("s.survey_score") <= 5), 1)
    .otherwise(0).alias("churn_risk_flag")
)

#Step 5: Handle Duplicates (Important)

#If multiple records per employee (e.g., multiple reviews)

from pyspark.sql.window import Window
from pyspark.sql.functions import row_number

window_spec = Window.partitionBy("emp_sk").orderBy(col("review_date").desc())

fact_df = fact_df.withColumn("rn", row_number().over(window_spec)) \
                 .filter(col("rn") == 1) \
                 .drop("rn")

#Step 6: Write to Gold Layer
fact_df.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics")

%%sql
--Attrition Rate
SELECT 
    'Attrition Rate' as metrics_name, 
    COUNT(CASE WHEN attrition_flag = 1 THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS metrics_value
FROM hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics;

%%sql
--Resignation Drivers
SELECT exit_reason, COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM hranalytics_db.silver_exit
GROUP BY exit_reason
ORDER BY cnt DESC;

%%sql
--Engagement Score
SELECT AVG(survey_score) AS avg_engagement
FROM hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics;

--eNPS Score
SELECT 
    (COUNT(CASE WHEN survey_score >= 9 THEN 1 END) -
     COUNT(CASE WHEN survey_score <= 6 THEN 1 END)) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS eNPS
FROM hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics;

--Early Churn Detection
SELECT emp_sk
FROM hranalytics_db.fact_employee_metrics
WHERE rating < 2 AND survey_score < 5;

    OrderId INT,

)

    OrderId INT,
    Product STRING,
    Amount DOUBLE
)
USING PARQUET;

%%sql

--Create delta Managed Table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inceptez_salesdb.Orders_delta (
    OrderId INT,
    Product STRING,
    Amount DOUBLE
)
USING DELTA;
